quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 11 Quiz 7
quiz.subtitle: 11.7: Graphs of Tangent, Cotangent, Secant, and Cosecant
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Graphing reciprocal and quotient trig functions

questions.question: Find the exact value of $\tan\left(\frac{3\pi}{4}\right)$.
questions.explanation: QII: tangent negative. Reference angle $\pi/4$, $\tan(\pi/4) = 1$. So $\tan(3\pi/4) = -1$.

questions.question: What is the period of the function $y = \tan(3x)$?
questions.explanation: Period for tangent is $\pi/b$. With $b = 3$, period $= \pi/3$.

questions.question: If a secant graph is shifted vertically up 5 units, what is the new range of $y = \sec(x) + 5$?
Find the smallest positive vertical asymptote of $y = \sec(2x)$.
questions.explanation: Asymptotes where $\cos(2x) = 0$: $2x = \pi/2$ → $x = \pi/4$. With $b=2$, asymptotes are twice as frequent.

questions.question: The function $y = \csc(x)$ has vertical asymptotes at $x = n\pi$. What are the asymptotes for $y = \csc(2x)$?
questions.explanation: Compression by factor 2 moves asymptotes closer: $x = n\pi/2$.

questions.question: Find the exact value of $\cot\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)$.
questions.explanation: $\cot(\pi/2) = \cos(\pi/2)/\sin(\pi/2) = 0/1 = 0$. Unlike tangent, cotangent is zero at $\pi/2$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You understand the reciprocal trig functions — evaluating tangent, cotangent, secant, and cosecant at key angles, finding periods, determining shifted ranges, and locating asymptotes.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Key reminders: (1) $\tan = \sin/\cos$, $\cot = \cos/\sin$, $\sec = 1/\cos$, $\csc = 1/\sin$, (2) Asymptotes occur where the denominator is zero, (3) Period of tangent/cotangent is $\pi/b$, period of secant/cosecant is $2\pi/b$.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Using reference angles and quadrant signs for all six trig functions, (2) $\cot = 1/\tan$ — when tangent is undefined, cotangent is zero, (3) For secant/cosecant, the range is always $(-\infty, -|a|+k] \cup [|a|+k, \infty)$.
scoreMessages.0: Review reciprocal trig functions: (1) $\tan\theta = \sin\theta/\cos\theta$, $\cot\theta = \cos\theta/\sin\theta$, (2) $\sec\theta = 1/\cos\theta$, $\csc\theta = 1/\sin\theta$, (3) Asymptotes of tangent: $\cos\theta = 0$; cotangent: $\sin\theta = 0$, (4) Period: tangent/cotangent = $\pi/b$, secant/cosecant = $2\pi/b$.
